Title: 24th International Workshop on Configuration (CONFWS'22).
Judge reason: The text describes a workshop focused on design-time configuration research (knowledge-representation formalisms, feature models, configurators, and reasoning methods) aimed at developing and maintaining configuration models rather than diagnosing or repairing misconfigurations in deployed/running systems by operators. It targets researchers, configurator vendors, and product-line engineers, not post-deployment operators troubleshooting live services.
Venue_name: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
Authors: Cipriano Forza; José A. Galindo; Martin Stettinger; Élise Vareilles

Title: BEEHIVE: behaviour-induced configuration of high variability-intensive systems
Judge reason: The paper studies design-time, simulation-based configuration of Cyber-Physical Systems using Simulink models by engineers, exploring parameter ranges and injecting noises/disturbances in models; it analyzes variants before deployment rather than operating on running systems. There is no evidence of operator-focused diagnosis, localization, or repair of misconfigurations in production/deployed systems.
Venue_name: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
Authors: Valeria Trombetta

Title: Acapulco: an extensible tool for identifying optimal and consistent feature model configurations
Judge reason: The paper studies design-time/product-line feature model configuration and optimization (artifact: feature models and derived variants) within FeatureIDE, targeting domain/application engineers and researchers rather than operators of running systems; it does not address post-deployment/runtime misconfiguration diagnosis or repair in operational environments.
Venue_name: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
Authors: Jabier Martinez; Daniel Strüber; José Miguel Horcas; Alexandru Burdusel; Steffen Zschaler

Title: Configuration of domotic systems based on constraint solving
Judge reason: The paper focuses on pre-deployment planning: it presents a constraint-solving configurator used by end-users and local service providers to plan and select smart-home components and explicitly states it is not about run-time/usage. The actor is the consumer/installer and the artifact is the planned configuration of devices (voltages, connectivity, wiring), not troubleshooting of deployed systems.
Venue_name: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
Authors: Gerhard Leitner; Martin Stettinger

Title: Applying incremental answer set solving to product configuration
Judge reason: The paper studies product configuration in industrial/customizable products using incremental Answer Set Programming and models user actions and deterministic configuration algorithms; it addresses constructing valid configurations of domain artifacts (racks, frames, modules) in an interactive/design-time setting rather than diagnosing or repairing configurations in running/production systems by operators.
Venue_name: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
Authors: Richard Comploi-Taupe; Giulia Francescutto; Gottfried Schenner

Title: ConGuess: a learning environment for configuration tasks
Judge reason: The paper focuses on an educational Android app for students/learners to practice constraint-solving and configuration semantics using generated CSP puzzles, i.e., a pre-deployment learning context rather than post-deployment operational troubleshooting. The actors are learners not operators and the artifacts are generated CSP instances, not real-world running system configuration files.
Venue_name: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
Authors: Andreas Hofbauer; Alexander Felfernig

Title: AI-implemented toolkit to assist users with career "configuration" the case of create your own future
Judge reason: The paper investigates a web-based AI career 'service configurator' for individual job seekers and coaches, focusing on user skill assessment and personalized career roadmaps rather than post-deployment software configuration or operational troubleshooting. The actors are learners and human advisors and the artifacts are career profiles, AI mentors, and training recommendations -not runtime system config files or production incidents.
Venue_name: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
Authors: Chiara Grosso; Noorie Sazen; Roberto Boselli

Title: A meta-model for product configuration ontologies
Judge reason: The paper focuses on design-time conceptual modelling of product configuration via ontologies and a meta-model for configuration models and configurators, referencing artefacts like ontologies and configuration models and actors such as developers, domain experts, and customers; it does not study post-deployment runtime incidents, operators, or troubleshooting of live system configuration files.
Venue_name: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
Authors: Ebrahim Khalil Abbasi; Tony Leclercq; Patrick Heymans

Title: Consistency-based integration of multi-stakeholder recommender systems with feature model configuration
Judge reason: The paper focuses on design-time feature model configuration and multi-stakeholder preference aggregation (modeling feature selections as CSPs and computing repairs), addressing product-line/configurator scenarios rather than post-deployment runtime incidents. It targets stakeholders using configurators to make joint decisions over feature models, not operators diagnosing misconfigurations in running systems or production configs.
Venue_name: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
Authors: Viet-Man Le; Thi Ngoc Trang Tran; Alexander Felfernig

Title: Knowledge-based configuration of videos using feature models
Judge reason: The paper focuses on feature-model based, user-driven configuration of personalized videos (pre-delivery content synthesis) involving end users and CSP encodings of feature selections; it does not study operators diagnosing or repairing runtime configuration faults in deployed systems or configuration files in production.
Venue_name: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
Authors: Sebastian Lubos; Markus Tautschnig; Alexander Felfernig; Viet-Man Le

Title: Configuration manager: describing an emerging professional figure
Judge reason: The paper studies the Configuration Manager professional role, competencies, and training for implementing and managing product configurators and mass-customization systems, focusing on organizational design and pre-deployment implementation/maintenance rather than post-deployment operator troubleshooting. The primary actors are managers/trainers and the artifacts are configurator implementations and business processes, not runtime incident diagnosis in deployed systems.
Venue_name: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
Authors: Enrico Sandrin; Cipriano Forza; Gerhard Leitner; Alessio Trentin

Title: Interactive feature modeling with background knowledge for validation and configuration
Judge reason: The paper focuses on design-time interactive feature modeling and validation for product engineers in a manufacturing/software product line context, explicitly describing model editing, background knowledge in first-order logic, and interactive configuration rather than post-deployment runtime troubleshooting by operators. It targets creating and validating designs before deployment, not diagnosing or repairing misconfigurations in running systems.
Venue_name: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
Authors: Simon Vandevelde; Benjamin Callewaert; Joost Vennekens

Title: A generic knowledge model for resource reconfiguration in the context of reconfigurable manufacturing systems
Judge reason: The paper explicitly targets reconfiguration during the exploitation (post-deployment) lifetime of Reconfigurable Manufacturing Systems and describes operators/decision-makers and MES/ERP as actors using the model to reconfigure running manufacturing resources and their settings. It treats real-time, operational configuration (resource settings, flows, reconfiguration time) rather than design-time variability or compile-time checks.
Venue_name: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
Authors: Mathis Allibe; Abdourahim Sylla; Gülgün Alpan

Title: Black-box optimization in a configuration system
Judge reason: The paper studies pre-deployment product configuration in the Merlin CPQ system: manufacturers/suppliers and end-users define objective functions and search for optimal variant selections during configuration/quotation time, not operators troubleshooting live/production systems. It does not address diagnosing, localizing, or repairing misconfigurations in running systems or operator workflows.
Venue_name: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
Authors: Maximilian Kucher; Tomás Balyo; Noemi Christensen

Title: On the Reuse of Existing Configurations for Testing Evolving Feature Models
Judge reason: The paper studies evolving feature models and reuse of combinatorial test suites for Software Product Lines, focusing on model-based test generation and artefacts (feature models and test suites) handled by testers/developers; it does not address runtime/production misconfiguration diagnosis or operator troubleshooting of deployed systems.
Venue_name: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
Authors: Andrea Bombarda; Silvia Bonfanti; Angelo Gargantini

Title: Should I Bother? Fast Patch Filtering for Statically-Configured Software Variants
Judge reason: The paper targets post-deployment patch management for 'shipped variants' and explicitly integrates into the patch-management lifecycle after deployment (it collects a Line-Range DB during variant deployment and runs checks when patches arrive), aimed at maintainers/operators handling deployed artifacts. It operates on real deployed/compiled artifacts (CPP-managed C/C++ source for shipped variants) rather than on design-time configuration models.
Venue_name: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
Authors: Tobias Landsberg; Christian Dietrich; Daniel Lohmann

Title: On the Use of Multi-valued Decision Diagrams to Count Valid Configurations of Feature Models
Judge reason: The paper addresses design-time analysis of Software Product Lines by compiling feature models into Multi-Valued Decision Diagrams to count valid products; it operates on feature models and benchmarks (synthetic and industrial) rather than on deployed/running systems. The actors are model engineers/researchers and the activity is knowledge compilation/model analysis, not post-deployment operator troubleshooting or runtime misconfiguration diagnosis.
Venue_name: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
Authors: Andrea Bombarda; Angelo Gargantini

Title: Towards a Product Configuration Representation for the Universal Variability Language
Judge reason: The paper addresses product configuration/derivation in Software Product Line engineering using variability models (UVL), which is a design-time modeling and tool support activity rather than post-deployment operational troubleshooting. The actors are engineers configuring UVL models and the artifacts are variability model representations and configuration sequences, not runtime configuration files or operator incident diagnosis in deployed systems.
Venue_name: ACM International Systems and Software Product Line Conference
Authors: Hafiyyan Sayyid Fadhlillah; Rick Rabiser
